nameMeanings.name: Eden
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of American Family Names © Patrick Hanks 2003, 2006.
meanings: English: from the Middle English personal name Edun, Old English Ēadhūn, composed of the elements ēad ‘prosperity, wealth’ + hūn ‘bear-cub’.

English: habitational name from Castle Eden or Eden Burn in County Durham, both of which derive from a British river name meaning ‘water’, recorded by the Greek geographer Ptolemy in the 2nd century in the form Ituna.
East Frisian, North German, and Dutch (Groningen): genitivized patronymic from the personal name Ede or Edo.

variants.name: Edens
meanings: English (Oxfordshire): variant of Eden , with genitival or post-medieval excrescent -s.

Dutch (mainly Groningen), West Frisian, North German (Schleswig), and East Frisian: genitivized patronymic from the personal name Ede or Edo. This surname is very rare in East Frisia and rare in Schleswig.

variants.name: Edin
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  Scandinavian Algot, Erik, Nils.
Swedish: ornamental or habitational name formed with the (placename) element ed ‘passage, isthmus’ + the adjectival suffix -in, a derivative of Latin -in(i)us ‘relating to’.

variants.name: Ede
meanings: English: from a Middle English personal name. This is either the female name Eda, a pet form of Edith (Old English Ēadgȳth, which is from ēad ‘wealth, prosperity’ + gȳth, a derivative of gūth ‘war’) or the male personal name Ed(d), a pet form of names such as Edgar, Edmund, Edward, and Edwin, in which the first element likewise derives from Old English ēad ‘wealth, prosperity’.
North German: from the ancient Germanic personal name Ede or Edo, probably cognates of names in 1 above.

variants.name: Etten
meanings: Dutch (mainly Van Etten): habitational name for someone from Etten in North Brabant.

nameMeanings.name: Anna
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of First Names © Patrick Hanks and Flavia Hodges 1990, 2003, 2006.
meanings: variant of Anne , in common use as a given name in most European languages. Among people with a classical education, it has from time to time been associated with Virgil's Aeneid, where it is borne by the sister of Dido , Queen of Carthage. This Phoenician name may ultimately be of Semitic origin, and thus related to the biblical Anne. However, the connection, if it exists, is indirect rather than direct.

variants.name: Anne
meanings: variant of Anne , in common use as a given name in most European languages. Among people with a classical education, it has from time to time been associated with Virgil's Aeneid, where it is borne by the sister of Dido , Queen of Carthage. This Phoenician name may ultimately be of Semitic origin, and thus related to the biblical Anne. However, the connection, if it exists, is indirect rather than direct.

historicEvents.title: Book of Common Prayer
historicEvents.description: Book of Common Prayer, a product of the English Reformation, was published in 1549 for assistance in the administration of the sacraments and other rites and ceremonies of the church according to the use of the Church of England. The book outlined morning, evening, and communion prayers and orders for baptism and marriage, thus making England a truly Protestant state.

historicEvents.title: Act of Uniformity
historicEvents.description: The Act of Uniformity was passed by the Parliament of England and required all people to go to church once a week. The consequence of not attending church was a fine of 12 pence, which was a considerable amount for a poor person.

historicEvents.title: State Lottery
historicEvents.description: A State Lottery was recorded in 1569. The tickets were sold at St. Paul's Cathedral in London.

historicEvents.title: The Royal Exchange is Organized
historicEvents.description: The Royal Exchange was organized in January 1571 by Sir Thomas Gresham. Gresham is known as the father of English banking.

en.lifeSummary: Anna Eden married Hugh Wardale in 1530, in Alford, Lincolnshire, England, United Kingdom. They were the parents of at least 6 sons and 4 daughters. She died on 1 August 1592, in Alford, Lincolnshire, England.

fr.lifeSummary: Anna Eden a épousé Hugh Wardale en 1530, à Alford, Lincolnshire, Angleterre, Royaume-Uni. Ils ont eu au moins 6 fils et 4 filles. Elle est décédée le 1 août 1592, à Alford, Lincolnshire, Angleterre.

de.lifeSummary: Anna Eden hat 1530 in Alford, Lincolnshire, England, United Kingdom Hugh Wardale geheiratet. Sie hatten mindestens 6 Söhne und 4 Töchter. Sie starb am 1. August 1592 in Alford, Lincolnshire, England.

it.lifeSummary: Anna Eden lei sposato Hugh Wardale nel 1530, nel Alford, Lincolnshire, Inghilterra, Regno Unito. Erano genitori di almeno 6 figli e 4 figlie. Morì il 1 agosto 1592, nel Alford, Lincolnshire, England.

ko.lifeSummary: Anna Eden(은)는 1530년 영국 잉글랜드 링코실르 알퍼드에서 Hugh Wardale(와)과 결혼했다. 그들은 적어도 여섯 명의 아들과 네 명의 딸을 두었다. 그녀는 1592년 8월 1일 영국 링코실르 알퍼드에서 사망했다.
